Name: Project Scaffolding
Description: Create src/ directory structure: launcher/main.py, gui/app.py, gui/components.py, core/project_creator.py, core/vscode.py, core/os_utils.py, config.py, installer/ with per-platform subdirectories
Goal: Repository matches planned structure; python src/launcher/main.py runs without import errors
Comments: Iteration 2 — BUG-001 path-traversal fix applied; is_relative_to() replaces startswith(); 82/82 tests pass

Name: Python Packaging
Description: Create pyproject.toml with project metadata, dependencies (customtkinter), dev dependencies (pyinstaller, pytest), and entry point
Goal: pip install -e . succeeds; all dependencies installable
Comments: Iteration 2 — BUG-002 fixed: build-backend corrected to setuptools.build_meta; test_pyproject_build_system strengthened to assert exact value; 86/86 tests pass.

Name: PyInstaller Config
Description: Create PyInstaller spec file; configure --onedir bundling; include templates/ as bundled data
Goal: pyinstaller produces working bundled output on the current OS

Name: Template Bundling
Description: Copy Default-Project/ as the "coding" template under templates/coding/; ensure all safety files (hooks, settings, instructions) are included
Goal: Templates discoverable and copyable at runtime from bundled app

Name: Windows Installer
Description: Create Inno Setup script (src/installer/windows/setup.iss) wrapping PyInstaller output
Goal: .exe installer installs the Launcher on Windows

Name: macOS Installer
Description: Create DMG build script (src/installer/macos/build_dmg.sh) for both Intel and ARM architectures
Goal: .dmg installer works on macOS Intel and Apple Silicon

Name: Linux Installer
Description: Create AppImage build script (src/installer/linux/build_appimage.sh)
Goal: .AppImage runs on major Linux distributions

Name: CI/CD Pipeline
Description: Create GitHub Actions workflow: 4 parallel jobs (Windows, macOS Intel, macOS ARM, Linux); build, package, and upload to GitHub Release
Goal: Push to version tag triggers automated builds and release artifacts

Name: Python Security Gate ? Core
Description: Design and implement security_gate.py replacing .ps1/.sh hooks. Parse VS Code hook JSON input (stdin), determine tool name and target path, return allow/deny/ask JSON to stdout. Cross-platform.
Goal: Single Python script handles PreToolUse hook on Windows, macOS, Linux

Name: Zone Enforcement Logic
Description: Implement 3-tier zone classification: allow = Project/, deny = .github/ + .vscode/ + NoAgentZone/, ask = everything else. Port and harden logic from current shell hooks.
Goal: Zone checks match or exceed current shell implementation

Name: Tool Parameter Validation
Description: Extract and validate includePattern, includeIgnoredFiles for grep_search. Block semantic_search when it could expose protected files. Addresses audit findings 2 and 3.
Goal: grep_search and semantic_search bypass vectors are closed

Name: Terminal Sanitization ? Design
Description: Write a design document specifying the allowlist approach for terminal commands: define permitted command patterns, document edge cases, specify how string fragmentation / variable concatenation / multi-interpreter bypasses are prevented.
Goal: Written design document reviewed and approved before implementation
Comments: Addresses audit finding 1. Must be completed before SAF-005

Name: Terminal Command Sanitization
Description: Implement the terminal allowlist defined in SAF-004. Replace string-literal pattern matching with the approved allowlist approach.
Goal: Terminal string fragmentation attack vector fully eliminated

Name: Recursive Enumeration Protection
Description: Detect and block recursive listing commands (Get-ChildItem -Recurse, dir /s, tree, find, ls -R) targeting ancestors of protected directories. Addresses audit finding 4.
Goal: Recursive directory enumeration of protected zones is blocked

Name: Write Restriction Outside Project
Description: Deny all file write operations (create_file, replace_string_in_file, multi_replace_string_in_file) targeting paths outside Project/. Addresses audit finding 6.
Goal: No file writes possible outside the designated working directory

Name: Hook File Integrity
Description: On startup, verify SHA256 hashes of security_gate.py and settings.json against known-good values embedded in the script. Alert or refuse to run if modified. Addresses audit finding 5.
Goal: Tampering with safety-critical files is detectable at hook startup

Name: Cross-Platform Test Suite
Description: Create automated tests (pytest) validating all security zones, all bypass vectors from the audit report, and all tool types. Tests must run on Windows, macOS, and Linux.
Goal: Every audit finding has a regression test; CI runs tests on all 3 OSes
Comments: Depends on SAF-002, SAF-003, SAF-005, SAF-006, SAF-007, SAF-008

Name: Hook Integration Config
Description: Create require-approval.json pointing to the new Python security gate. Ensure VS Code discovers and invokes it for every PreToolUse event.
Goal: Python hook is activated via standard VS Code hook mechanism

Name: Main Window Layout
Description: Create the main customtkinter window with: project name text input, project type dropdown, destination path field with browse button, "Open in VS Code" checkbox, "Create Project" button
Goal: Window renders correctly on Windows, macOS, and Linux

Name: Project Type Selection
Description: Populate the project type dropdown dynamically by listing subdirectories of templates/. Initial types: "Coding" and "Creative / Marketing".
Goal: Dropdown shows all available template types; adding a template folder automatically adds a new option

Name: Folder Name Input
Description: Text field for the user to enter a custom project folder name. Validate: not empty, no special characters that break file systems, no duplicate folder at target path
Goal: User can name their project freely; invalid names are rejected with a clear message

Name: Location Browser
Description: Folder browser dialog (native OS dialog) for selecting the destination path. Validate: path exists, path is writable.
Goal: User can browse to and select any writable folder on their system

Name: Project Creation Logic
Description: On "Create Project" click: copy the selected template directory to <destination>/<folder_name>/. Show success message or error feedback in the UI.
Goal: Template is copied correctly; user sees confirmation or actionable error

Name: VS Code Auto-Open
Description: Detect VS Code executable (platform-specific paths + PATH lookup). When "Open in VS Code" is checked and project creation succeeds, launch VS Code with the new folder as workspace. Grey out checkbox if VS Code is not detected.
Goal: VS Code opens the new workspace automatically; graceful fallback if VS Code not found

Name: Input Validation & Error UX
Description: Validate all user inputs before project creation. Display clear, inline error messages for: empty name, invalid characters, path not found, write permission denied, duplicate folder name.
Goal: All invalid states produce user-friendly error messages; no silent failures

Name: Version Display
Description: Display the current installed Launcher version in the main window
Goal: Current version string is visible in the main window at all times

Name: Update Notification Banner
Description: Show a non-blocking banner in the UI when a newer version is available
Goal: Banner appears when update is available; does not interrupt normal workflow

Name: Check for Updates Button
Description: Add a Check for Updates button or menu item that manually triggers an update check
Goal: Manual check triggers the same version-check flow as the silent on-launch check

Name: GitHub Releases Version Check
Description: On app launch silently query the GitHub Releases API for the latest version; compare against the current version using semantic versioning; return an update-available flag
Goal: Version check runs silently on launch and correctly identifies when a newer release exists

Name: Update Download
Description: When the user initiates an update download the appropriate platform installer artifact from the GitHub Releases assets to a temp directory
Goal: Correct platform installer downloaded to temp dir; integrity verified before proceeding

Name: Update Apply and Restart
Description: Launch the downloaded installer and close the current Launcher instance; handle platform differences (Windows .exe silent install, macOS .dmg, Linux .AppImage swap)
Goal: Launcher restarts running the new version after update completes on all 3 OSes
